question.text: Under the SEBI Act, what power does SEBI have regarding stock exchange bye-laws?

answer.text: SEBI has the power to make or amend bye-laws of recognized stock exchanges
SEBI can only suggest changes but cannot enforce them
Stock exchanges have full autonomy over their bye-laws without SEBI involvement
Only the Central Government can amend stock exchange bye-laws

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the SEBI Act and related provisions, SEBI has the power to make or amend bye-laws of recognized stock exchanges to ensure they align with regulatory objectives and investor protection goals. **The Trap:** While stock exchanges can propose bye-laws, SEBI has overriding authority to modify them. This ensures regulatory consistency across all exchanges. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Stock exchange bye-laws cover trading hours, circuit breakers, margin requirements, and member obligations. SEBI's power over bye-laws ensures uniform market standards.
